capabilities: Implement seed and version pinning in test environments to ensure consistent and reproducible test results across different executions.

procedure.action: Set a fixed random seed in test configurations by using a constant value in the random number generator initialization, ensuring deterministic behavior across test runs using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Pin software dependencies to specific versions by using a package manager lock file (e.g., package-lock.json, Pipfile.lock) to prevent version drift in test environments using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Capture the test specification by using domain-appropriate tooling, ensuring the specification is stored in a version-controlled repository for traceability.
Execute tests with the pinned configuration and assert that error_count < 1 and result_valid = true, logging operation_duration_ms and result_code to the audit trail.

Assert the test procedure output by using domain-appropriate tooling, ensuring that all outputs meet predefined success criteria and are logged for audit purposes by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

summary: Ensure test reproducibility by pinning software versions and using consistent seed values, enabling reliable evaluation of derived knowledge bases.
title: Test Reproducibility

failureConditions: Test results become inconsistent when random seed values are not fixed, causing variability in test outcomes and unreliable evaluations.
Version drift occurs when dependencies are not pinned, leading to discrepancies in test environments and causing test failures.
Audit trails become incomplete when operation_duration_ms and result_code are not logged, causing difficulties in tracing test executions.
metrics: that error_count < 1 and result_valid = true, logging operation_duration_ms and result_code to the audit trail
the test procedure output by using domain-appropriate tooling, ensuring that all outputs meet predefined success criteri
successConditions: artifact_hash matches keccak256(JCS(envelope)); assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
